nouns.summary: The text places the events “after the exile/migration,” indicating that the births that follow occur following this period of exile.
Babylon is referenced through the phrase “the exile of Babylon,” establishing that the generational events occur after Babylon’s exile/migration period.
Jechoniah is said to have begotten (fathered) Σαλαθιήλ in the sequence of the genealogy.
Salathiel is identified as the son who is begotten by Ἰεχονίας, and he then begot Ζοροβαβέλ.
Zerubbabel is described as the child begotten by Σαλαθιήλ within the genealogy sequence.
